summary: High‑energy Spring ’89 show with a smoking Scarlet > Fire and solid audience recording despite occasional dropouts
review: The performance captures the excitement of the Dead’s early‑Spring ’89 tour – Jerry is on fire, the band drives through new material while delivering a standout Scarlet Begonias → Fire sequence and a bright Standing on the Moon. The audience tape is generally clear and lively, though listeners note occasional right‑channel dropouts and a few muddy sections.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating ~4.0 with many 4–5‑star comments praising the setlist, energy and Jerry’s performance; recording issues are mentioned but do not dominate the overall impression.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear audience mix with good crowd presence; occasional right‑channel dropout early in Half Step and some muddiness on a few tracks. Mickey’s percussive “clap” is audible and sometimes described as electronic.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set rolls out new material (Mississippi, Built to Last, We Can Run) with high crowd energy; the second set builds momentum into a fiery Scarlet > Fire jam and carries through with strong Lovelight and Standing on the Moon. Overall flow is upbeat, though a few new songs feel tentative.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocal and guitar work are especially lively on Scarlet > Fire and Touch; reviewers note his animated stage presence and powerful vocal shouts.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is solid and occasionally highlighted for its low‑end power; Phil is mentioned laughing with Bobby during Lovelight.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby (Weir) delivers a strong Lovelight performance and memorable on‑stage antics, including moving between the drums and audience.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboardist) adds background vocal harmonies on Lovelight; occasional missed lyric moments on new songs are noted.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s percussive claps (including an electronic “clap”) are frequently mentioned; drum work described as fluid and tribal during the jam.
